vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1621

plan.title: feat(skills,coding): deft-directive-debug — systematic root-cause investigation skill + coding/debugging.md (supersedes #659, #1173)

narratives.Description: feat(skills,coding): deft-directive-debug — systematic root-cause investigation skill + coding/debugging.md (supersedes #659, #1173)
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Directive has no debugging/root-cause capability. When agents get stuck they thrash, retry randomly, fix-before-understanding, or treat the first plausible hypothesis as correct. This issue **consolidates** the two previously-fragmented halves of that gap into one tracked feature:

- **#659** — `feat(skills): add deft-debug skill` (the *workflow* surface)
- **#1173** — `feat(coding): add coding/debugging.md` (the *coding-standard* surface)

Both descend from the same obra/superpowers `systematic-debugging` pattern and were never picked up. This issue supersedes both and **enriches** the design with a concrete, battle-tested reference implementation: the `forensic-research` skill bundle (origin: SLizard), which adds mandatory falsification, popularity traps, a deterministic validator, a vBRIEF investigation ledger, and observability-gap output on top of the basic phase/gate loop.

`#659` and `#1173` will be closed as `not planned` (superseded) when this is filed.

## Why one issue (not two)

A skill and its backing coding-standard are a **pair** — directive already ships them coupled (cf. #1471: review-cycle skill ↔ `coding/review.md`). As two issues they fragmented the work and created "which one do I implement?" ambiguity. One issue, multiple deliverables, decomposes cleanly into stories at build time.

## Deliverables

### D0 — Vendor the reference bundle (prereq)
Vendor the `forensic-research` bundle verbatim (SLizard quarantine intact) into `docs/reference/forensic-research/` as read-only source material, with a `VENDORED.md` provenance header. All in-repo links below resolve against this.

### D1 — `coding/debugging.md` (the rules)
The canonical root-cause coding standard, RFC2119-tiered:
- **Iron Law**: no fixes without root-cause investigation first.
- **Four phases** with hard gates: Reproduce → Isolate/Pattern-analyze → Hypothesize → Verify (implement only after the cause is confirmed with evidence).
- **3-fix architecture gate**: after 3 failed distinct fixes, STOP and escalate to architectural review.
- **Multi-component rule**: add diagnostic instrumentation at every boundary before proposing fixes.
- **Forensic-rigor rules** (from the reference bundle): falsification-before-fixation, `config-is-not-code` (runtime proof required), proof-required-disproval ("no evidence" → unknown, never failed), the tautology trap ("slow because it timed out" is not an answer).
- **Fact vs Hypothesis labeling** aligned with #1580 (shared vocabulary; #1580 stays open and owns the review-cycle/triage findings-format surface).
- Rationalization table.

### D2 — `skills/deft-directive-debug/` (the workflow)
A skill that operationalizes D1, authored fresh (genericized from the vendored reference — do not repurpose the vendored copy as the live skill):
- Trigger keywords: "debug", "I'm stuck", "root cause", "investigate why", "systematic debugging".
- Wave workflow: traps → branch investigators → mechanism drill-down → **falsifier** → **red-team** → synthesizer.
- Sub-agent dispatch prompts with strict per-role **write boundaries** (orchestrator prefetches evidence; sub-agents interpret + cite).
- **vBRIEF investigation ledger** (`x-investigation` / `x-claim` / `EV-*` references) — must conform to the #1620 x-namespacing rule.
- Pluggable **domain packs** (generic core + per-project pack).
- **§2b observability-gap output**: every investigation emits "what to log next time" so the next one is definitive.

### D3 — `task verify:investigation` gate (in scope)
Promote the reference bundle's prose validator into a deterministic gate (hard-failures vs soft-warnings; three-state exit), wired into `task check`. Backs the determinism-tier ladder (#634).

## Files affected

- `docs/reference/forensic-research/**` (vendored, D0)
- `coding/debugging.md` (new, D1) + `coding/coding.md` cross-reference
- `skills/deft-directive-debug/**` (new, D2) + AGENTS.md/template skill-routing entry
- `scripts/` + `tasks/` + tests for the validator gate (D3)
- `tests/content/` coverage for the new coding rule

## Related / not folded in

- **#1580** (Fact vs Judgment labeling) — **stays open**; D1/D2 adopt its vocabulary, but its review-cycle + triage findings-format surface is independent.
- #634 (determinism-tier ladder) — D3 backs this.
- #1620 (vBRIEF x-namespacing) — the investigation ledger must conform.
- #1396 (structured agent decision log) — shares the ledger DNA.
- Carries forward #1173's prior "related": #1171 (errors as instructions), #1164 (test integrity).

## Source

obra/superpowers `systematic-debugging` (166K stars) + the `forensic-research` skill bundle (origin: SLizard, bundled 2026-06-13), vendored under `docs/reference/forensic-research/` by D0.
